title: Transfection with PEI
before_start: {"blocks":[{"key":"cv3ae","text":"Prepare a clean space in the biosafety cabinet with all the reagents and a empty waste container and warm media","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
warning: {"blocks":[{"key":"bh9g3","text":"PEI penetrates cells and is a significant irritant to skin and mucus membranes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
materials_text: {"blocks":[{"key":"3h8ni","text":"Polyethyleneimine (PEI, linear MW 25,000) in 1 milligram per milliliter solution","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"ceiml","text":"serum-free media appropriate for your cells","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"asdu9","text":"Purified endotoxin-free plasmid DNA ~100 micrograms","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"7o3l","text":"Complete media appropriate for your cells","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"48uei","text":"Sterile Eppendorf tubes and cell culture dishes","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"atngq","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}
steps: Cells should be 50-70% confluency and have a healthy morphology
In a clean biosafety cabinet, remove serum media from cells (DO NOT TRYPSINIZE!)
Add 1.5 mL of serum-free cell culture media (DMEM or similar)

In a sterile tube dilute total plasmid DNA (µg) in serum-free DMEM. 6 well plate: 200 µl + 3 µg of total DNA per well; 24 well plate : 30 µl + 0.5 µg of total DNA per well Add PEI (1 µg/µL) to the diluted DNA. Mix immediately by vortexing or pipetting. The volume of PEI used is based on a 3:1 ratio of PEI (µg):total DNA (µg). This ratio can be optimized to maximize transfection efficiency. a.6-well plate: 9 µl of PEI(1 µg/µl) = 9 µg PEI to 3 µg DNA b.You will need 200 µL serum-free DMEM + 3 µgDNA+9 µg PEI for each well of a 6-well plate you plan to transfect. c.24 well plate: 1.5 µl of PEI(1 µg/µl) = 1.5 µg PEI to 0.5 µg DNA d.You will need 30 µl serum-free DMEM + 0.5 µg DNA + 1.5 µg PEI for each well of a 24-well plate.
For the negative control, add 9 µg PEI to 200 µL serum-free media - no plasmid DNA.
Add DNA/PEI mixture to cells using the volume per well described above
Allow cells to incubate with DNA/PEI mixture for 4 hours in the 37°C cell culture incubator.
After 4 hours, remove serum-free medium with DNA/PEI mixture and replace with 2-3 mL complete serum media.
Incubate cells for 24-48 hours, at 37°C to allow for plasmid expression and protein translation
Fix or harvest for downstream analysis.
creator: JCPrice
